pattern: Bitmask DP: represent each string as a bitmask of character presence and build all valid (non-overlapping) subsequence bitmasks, tracking the maximum popcount.
hints: Strings with duplicate letters in themselves can never contribute to a valid concatenation; filter them out first.
Represent each valid string as a 26-bit integer where bit i means the i-th letter is present; two strings are compatible if their bitmasks have no bits in common.
Maintain a set of bitmasks for all reachable concatenations seen so far; for each new string, add its bitmask to every compatible existing bitmask and track the maximum bit count.

edge_inputs.description: no valid full concatenation due to overlap
multiple valid subsets of different sizes
string itself has duplicate so is invalid
best subset skips a string that overlaps with two others

stress.description: 16 short strings with distinct characters each
stress.generator_python:
def gen(rng, size):
    import string
    n = min(16, max(1, size))
    arr = []
    for _ in range(n):
        length = rng.randint(1, 4)
        chars = rng.sample(string.ascii_lowercase, length)
        arr.append(''.join(chars))
    return [arr]
